do_not_merge.reason: Different entity_name: McKissack vs Michael Baker International. Both are consultants but distinct firms. Shared acronym IEC is a role, not a unique identifier. Contradiction test: entity_name differs (test 1).
Different entity_type: consultant vs organization. Also entity_name differs (McKissack vs Independent Engineering Consultant). Contradiction test: entity_type differs (test 1).

Different entity_type: consultant vs organization. Also entity_name differs (Michael Baker International vs Independent Engineering Consultant). Despite shared identity key entity_independent-engineering-consultant, entity_type contradiction forces do_not_merge (test 1).

suggested_rules: When a role acronym (e.g., 'IEC') is used by multiple distinct entities, it does not imply identity; entity_name and entity_type are decisive. The existing rule 'Acronym + expanded legal name of the same org DO merge' applies only when the legal name matches, not when the acronym is a shared role.
suggestion.rationale: All three records represent distinct entities: McKissack (MTA Independent Engineering Consultant), Michael Baker International (also IEC), and a generic 'Independent Engineering Consultant' record. Strong identity fields (entity_name, entity_type) differ between each pair, satisfying the contradiction test (test 1) and forcing do_not_merge. The shared acronym IEC is a role descriptor, not a unique identifier.
